creator: bf@FreeBSD.org
text: I don't think that we need quibble about the commit message -- it is a vulnerability in the sense that successful deanonymization has been demonstrated under realistic conditions.  And I don't hesitate to make a positive recommendation to use randomization -- both the upstream developers and other authorities do so as well, and nearly every tor user ought to be concerned about this.  I'll reconsider the message, since I think there ought to be a warning about exceeding ipport_randomcps. But I want to keep it short, and I am not sure that the pkg-message of a port is the best place to instruct novices on how to make a persistent sysctl setting.
